2603.19676.title: ATHENA: Adaptive Test-Time Steering for Improving Count Fidelity in Diffusion Models
2603.19676.paper_summary: Text-to-image diffusion models achieve high visual fidelity but surprisingly exhibit systematic failures in numerical control when prompts specify explicit object counts. To address this limitation, we introduce ATHENA, a model-agnostic, test-time adaptive steering framework that improves object count fidelity without modifying model architectures or requiring retraining. ATHENA leverages intermediate representations during sampling to estimate object counts and applies count-aware noise corrections early in the denoising process, steering the generation trajectory before structural errors become difficult to revise. We present three progressively more advanced variants of ATHENA that trade additional computation for improved numerical accuracy, ranging from static prompt-based steering to dynamically adjusted count-aware control. Experiments on established benchmarks and a new visually and semantically complex dataset show that ATHENA consistently improves count fidelity, particularly at higher target counts, while maintaining favorable accuracy-runtime trade-offs across multiple diffusion backbones.
2603.19676.paper_summary_zh: 文本到圖像的擴散模型達到了高視覺保真度，但令人驚訝的是，當提示指定明確的物體數量時，卻出現了系統性的數值控制失敗。為了解決這一限制，我們引入了ATHENA，一種模型無關的測試時自適應引導框架，能在不修改模型架構或要求重新訓練的情況下改善物體數量的保真度。ATHENA在取樣過程中利用中間表示來估算物體數量，並在去噪過程的早期應用數量感知的噪聲修正，從而在結構性錯誤變得難以修正之前引導生成軌跡。我們提出了三種逐步更先進的ATHENA變體，這些變體在提高數值準確性方面以額外的計算為代價，範圍從靜態提示基礎的引導到動態調整的數量感知控制。在既有基準和一個新的視覺和語義複雜數據集上的實驗顯示，ATHENA在提高數量保真度方面始終表現出色，特別是在較高的目標數量下，同時在多個擴散骨幹上保持有利的準確性與運行時間的權衡。
2603.19676.author: Mohammad Shahab Sepehri et.al.
2603.19676.authors: Mohammad Shahab Sepehri, Asal Mehradfar, Berk Tinaz, Salman Avestimehr, Mahdi Soltanolkotabi
